design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate streets and Zuul Building to catch ghosts, earn money, buy upgrades, face boss encounters
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (money for upgrades) vs. escalating enemy difficulty, compounded by notoriously punishing staircase sequence blocking progression
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Become a Ghostbuster equipped to handle supernatural threats through preparation and combat
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Aggressively difficult conversion that punishes players with a grueling, nearly-impossible staircase sequence; forces resource management decisions but offers little margin for error; the game's difficulty is seen as a core design flaw rather than intentional challenge philosophy

difficulty.lives_system: Limited lives with game over condition
difficulty.checkpointing: Minimal; staircase sequence blocks most players from reaching final boss
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Extremely limited; difficulty is perceived as poor design rather than skill-based challenge; equipment upgrades provide marginal assistance

progression.upgrade_system: Money earned from defeating ghosts used to purchase weapons (positron gun variants, bombs), shields, and health items at shops
progression.unlock_mechanism: Progress through sequential screens and building interiors; staircase acts as hard gate to final content

world_building_techniques: Licensed IP (Ghostbusters brand and theme music)
Environmental setting (streets, buildings, Zuul Building as final destination)
Generic haunted locations as level backdrops

boss_design.philosophy: Boss encounters serve as level completions requiring defeat and capture; minimal mechanical depth
notable_bosses: Final boss (unspecified; almost never reached due to staircase difficulty)
Implied mid-level ghost encounters in Zuul Building

danger_signals: Staircase sequence as infamous difficulty spike preventing player progression
Ghost encounters escalate from manageable to overwhelming
environmental_storytelling: Zuul Building as central supernatural location
Multiple haunted buildings suggesting widespread ghost infestation
Shop presence (attributed to developers Workss in Japanese version) as survival necessity

sources_used: tcrf: The Cutting Room Floor - Ghostbusters (NES)
wikipedia: Ghostbusters (1990 video game) [Note: Wikipedia source provided contains 1990 Genesis game data, not 1988 NES data; used for general Ghostbusters mechanics context only]
